batchNotes: The stale hv3-038 critical-obligation description is fixed: it now requires only the requested reorder, matching the one-call slides_edit operation and the reorder-only rubric.

The batch now clearly meets the minimum of eight indirect or natural triggers. At least hv3-022, hv3-024, hv3-026, hv3-027, hv3-028, hv3-030, hv3-032, hv3-034, hv3-036, and hv3-039 express their main triggers through ordinary task facts rather than copying policy instructions.

All seven prior case-specific findings remain resolved. Post-tool artifact interpretation and preservation checks are appropriately diagnostic where the one-turn function-tool constraint prevents grading a later prose response.

Structural regression checks passed for all 20 cases: sequential IDs hv3-021 through hv3-040, valid critical-obligation references, at most one available tool per case, and tool expectation names present in the declared tool list.
No new high- or medium-severity issue was introduced by the second revision pass.
